heading: Secured loans
label: 603 Secured loans

children.label: A
children.text: to finance eligible project costs of any project selected under section 602;

children.label: B
children.text: to refinance interim construction financing of eligible project costs of any project selected under section 602;

children.label: C
children.text: to refinance existing Federal credit instruments for rural infrastructure projects; or

children.label: i
children.text: is selected under section 602; or

children.label: ii
children.text: otherwise meets the requirements of section 602.

children.heading: .—Limitation on refinancing of interim construction financing
children.label: 2 .—Limitation on refinancing of interim construction financing
A
children.text: if the maturity of such interim construction financing is later than 1 year after the substantial completion of the project; and

children.label: B
children.text: later than 1 year after the date of substantial completion of the project.

children.heading: .—Risk assessment
children.label: 3 .—Risk assessment
children.text: Before entering into an agreement under this subsection, the Secretary, in consultation with the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, shall determine an appropriate capital reserve subsidy amount for each secured loan, taking into account each rating letter provided by an agency under section 602(b)(3)(B).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: A secured loan under this section with respect to a project shall be on such terms and conditions and contain such covenants, representations, warranties, and requirements (including requirements for audits) as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the amount of a secured loan under this section shall not exceed the lesser of 49 percent of the reasonably anticipated eligible project costs or if the secured loan does not receive an investment grade rating, the amount of the senior project obligations.

children.heading: .—Rural projects fund
children.label: B .—Rural projects fund
children.text: In the case of a project capitalizing a rural projects fund, the maximum amount of a secured loan made to a State infrastructure bank shall be determined in accordance with section 602(a)(5)(B)(iii).

children.label: I
children.text: tolls;

children.label: II
children.text: user fees;

children.label: III
children.text: payments owing to the obligor under a public-private partnership;

children.label: IV
children.text: other dedicated revenue sources that also secure the senior project obligations; or

children.label: V
children.text: in the case of a secured loan for a project capitalizing a rural projects fund, any other dedicated revenue sources available to a State infrastructure bank, including repayments from loans made by the bank for rural infrastructure projects; and

children.label: ii
children.text: include a rate covenant, coverage requirement, or similar security feature supporting the project obligations; and

children.label: B
children.text: may have a lien on revenues described in subparagraph (A), subject to any lien securing project obligations.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraphs (B) and (C), the interest rate on a secured loan under this section shall be not less than the yield on United States Treasury securities of a similar maturity to the maturity of the secured loan on the date of execution of the loan agreement.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: The interest rate of a loan offered to a rural infrastructure project or a rural projects fund under the TIFIA program shall be at ½ of the Treasury Rate in effect on the date of execution of the loan agreement.

children.heading: .—Application
children.label: ii .—Application
children.text: The rate described in clause (i) shall only apply to any portion of a loan the subsidy cost of which is funded by amounts set aside for rural infrastructure projects and rural project funds under section 608(a)(3)(A).

children.label: i
children.text: 1½ percentage points (150 basis points); or

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of the increase in the interest rate.

children.label: i
children.text: 35 years after the date of substantial completion of the project; and

children.label: ii
children.text: if the useful life of the capital asset being financed is of a lesser period, the useful life of the asset.

children.heading: .—Rural projects fund
children.label: B .—Rural projects fund
children.text: In the case of a project capitalizing a rural projects fund, the final maturity date of the secured loan shall not exceed 35 years after the date on which the secured loan is obligated.

children.label: i
children.text: 75 years after the date of substantial completion of the project; or

children.label: ii
children.text: 75 percent of the estimated useful life of the capital asset.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the secured loan shall not be subordinated to the claims of any holder of project obligations in the event of bankruptcy, insolvency, or liquidation of the obligor.

children.label: I
children.text: the secured loan is rated in the A category or higher;

children.label: II
children.text: the secured loan is secured and payable from pledged revenues not affected by project performance, such as a tax-backed revenue pledge or a system-backed pledge of project revenues; and

children.label: III
children.text: the TIFIA program share of eligible project costs is 33 percent or less.

children.label: I
children.text: the maximum credit subsidy to be paid by the Federal Government shall be not more than 10 percent of the principal amount of the secured loan; and

children.label: II
children.text: the obligor shall be responsible for paying the remainder of the subsidy cost, if any.

children.heading: .—Fees
children.label: 7 .—Fees
children.text: The Secretary may establish fees at a level sufficient to cover all or a portion of the costs to the Federal Government of making a secured loan under this section.

children.heading: .—Non-federal share
children.label: 8 .—Non-federal share
children.text: The proceeds of a secured loan under the TIFIA program may be used for any non-Federal share of project costs required under this title or chapter 53 of title 49, if the loan is repayable from non-Federal funds.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The total Federal assistance provided for a project receiving a loan under the TIFIA program shall not exceed 80 percent of the total project cost.

children.heading: .—Rural projects fund
children.label: B .—Rural projects fund
children.text: A project capitalizing a rural projects fund shall satisfy subparagraph (A) through compliance with the Federal share requirement described in section 610(e)(3)(B).

children.label: A
children.text: the projected cash flow from project revenues and other repayment sources; and

children.label: B
children.text: the useful life of the project.

children.heading: .—Commencement
children.label: 2 .—Commencement
children.text: Scheduled loan repayments of principal or interest on a secured loan under this section shall commence not later than 5 years after the date of substantial completion of the project.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: If, at any time after the date of substantial completion of the project, the project is unable to generate sufficient revenues to pay the scheduled loan repayments of principal and interest on the secured loan, the Secretary may, subject to subparagraph (C), allow the obligor to add unpaid principal and interest to the outstanding balance of the secured loan.

children.label: i
children.text: continue to accrue interest in accordance with subsection (b)(4) until fully repaid; and

children.label: ii
children.text: be scheduled to be amortized over the remaining term of the loan.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Any payment deferral under subparagraph (A) shall be contingent on the project meeting criteria established by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Repayment standards
children.label: ii .—Repayment standards
children.text: The criteria established pursuant to clause (i) shall include standards for reasonable assurance of repayment.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), any excess revenues that remain after satisfying scheduled debt service requirements on the project obligations and secured loan and all deposit requirements under the terms of any trust agreement, bond resolution, or similar agreement securing project obligations may be applied annually to prepay the secured loan without penalty.

children.heading: .—Certain applicants
children.label: ii .—Certain applicants
children.text: In the case of a secured loan or other secured Federal credit instrument provided after the date of enactment of the Surface Transportation Reauthorization Act of 2021, if the obligor is a governmental entity, agency, or instrumentality, the obligor shall not be required to prepay the secured loan or other secured Federal credit instrument with any excess revenues described in clause (i) if the obligor enters into an agreement to use those excess revenues only for purposes authorized under this title or title 49.

children.heading: .—Use of proceeds of refinancing
children.label: B .—Use of proceeds of refinancing
children.text: The secured loan may be prepaid at any time without penalty from the proceeds of refinancing from non-Federal funding sources.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), as soon as practicable after substantial completion of a project and after notifying the obligor, the Secretary may sell to another entity or reoffer into the capital markets a secured loan for the project if the Secretary determines that the sale or reoffering can be made on favorable terms.

children.heading: .—Consent of obligor
children.label: 2 .—Consent of obligor
children.text: In making a sale or reoffering under paragraph (1), the Secretary may not change the original terms and conditions of the secured loan without the written consent of the obligor.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may provide a loan guarantee to a lender in lieu of making a secured loan under this section if the Secretary determines that the budgetary cost of the loan guarantee is substantially the same as that of a secured loan.

children.heading: .—Terms
children.label: 2 .—Terms
children.text: The terms of a loan guarantee under paragraph (1) shall be consistent with the terms required under this section for a secured loan, except that the rate on the guaranteed loan and any prepayment features shall be negotiated between the obligor and the lender, with the consent of the Secretary.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of the FAST Act, the Secretary shall make available an expedited application process or processes available at the request of entities seeking secured loans under the TIFIA program that use a set or sets of conventional terms established pursuant to this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the secured loan is in an amount of not greater than $100,000,000;

children.label: B
children.text: the secured loan is secured and payable from pledged revenues not affected by project performance, such as a tax-backed revenue pledge, tax increment financing, or a system-backed pledge of project revenues; and

children.label: C
children.text: repayment of the loan commences not later than 5 years after disbursement.

children.label: i
children.text: the terms under paragraph (2); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the additional criteria described in subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: The secured loan is made on terms and conditions that substantially conform to the conventional terms and conditions established by the National Surface Transportation Innovative Finance Bureau.

children.label: ii
children.text: The secured loan is rated in the A category or higher.

children.label: iii
children.text: The TIFIA program share of eligible project costs is 33 percent or less.

children.label: iv
children.text: The applicant demonstrates a reasonable expectation that the contracting process for the project can commence by not later than 90 days after the date on which a Federal credit instrument is obligated for the project under the TIFIA program.

children.label: v
children.text: The project has received a categorical exclusion, a finding of no significant impact, or a record of decision under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—Written notice
children.label: C .—Written notice
children.text: The Secretary shall provide to an applicant seeking a secured loan under the expedited decision process under this paragraph a written notice informing the applicant whether the Secretary has approved or disapproved the application by not later than 180 days after the date on which the Secretary submits to the applicant a letter indicating that the National Surface Transportation Innovative Finance Bureau has commenced the creditworthiness review of the project.
